roles.title: Learning Coordinator
roles.tagline: Own how the chapter learns together.
roles.summary: Plan and run the chapter's core learning programs — Study Jams, workshops, and skill sessions — so every session actually lands.
responsibilities: Plan and run the chapter's core learning programs, especially Study Jams.
Work with the Campus Lead to design and schedule sessions through the semester.
Track what's working and adjust format or pace based on turnout and feedback.
Pitch sessions right for whoever's in the room — from first-years to final-years.

question.label: Describe a topic or skill you'd want to run a session on, and how you'd structure it for beginners.
question.placeholder: e.g. Git & GitHub for first-timers — a 60-minute hands-on session where...

roles.title: Women in Tech Lead
roles.tagline: Make sure tech feels open to every woman on campus.
roles.summary: Champion gender diversity across the chapter and build a space where women feel comfortable showing up, asking questions, and taking the lead.
responsibilities: Champion gender diversity and inclusion across all chapter activities.
Build a space where women feel comfortable participating and taking the lead.
Plan initiatives, events, or partnerships that support women in tech on campus.
Represent the chapter in wider women-in-tech community efforts.

question.label: What's one barrier you've noticed that keeps women from participating in tech spaces on campus, and how would you address it?
question.placeholder: Share something you've actually observed, not just a general statement...

roles.title: Outreach Lead
roles.tagline: Get the word out and tell the chapter's stories.
roles.summary: Grow the chapter's visibility on campus, and spot and share the interesting things makers in the community are building.
responsibilities: Build and run outreach strategy to grow the chapter's visibility on campus and beyond.
Spot interesting projects and makers in the community and help tell their story.
Own the chapter's social presence — Instagram, LinkedIn, and campus promotion.
Build partnerships with other clubs, communities, and departments.

question.label: Pitch us a one-line idea for a post or campaign that would get students curious about TinkerHub.
question.placeholder: Keep it punchy — one sentence is enough.
